data.title: Paramount Skydance appoints Dennis Cinelli CFO
data.description:
Paramount Skydance (PSKY) said on Wednesday that Dennis Cinelli has been appointed CFO, effective January 15, 2026.

Cinelli, who succeeds interim CFO Andrew Warren, has resigned from the post of board director. Warren will continue to offer counsel as a strategic advisor.

Most recently, Cinelli served as CFO of Scale AI.

data.title: Citigroup outlines 5%–6% NII growth target for 2026 as transformation nears completion
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: Citigroup Inc. (C) Q4 2025

Management View

  • CEO Jane Fraser highlighted that "excluding the impact of a notable item, our adjusted EPS was $1.81, and our adjusted RoTCE was 7.7%." Fraser emphasized record revenues across all five business segments, improved returns, and a 180 basis point annual RoTCE improvement after adjusting for Banamex and Russia. Management underscored positive operating leverage for the second consecutive year and record performance in Services, Markets, Banking, Wealth, and U.S. Personal Banking. Fraser stated, "We repurchased over $13 billion in common shares during the year, including $4.5 billion in the fourth quarter as part of our $20 billion plan. Increasing our dividend resulted in a total capital return of over $17.5 billion, the most since the pandemic."
  • Fraser announced nearing the end of international divestitures, including the sale agreement for the consumer business in Poland and progress on the Russia exit. She also noted, "Over 80% of our programs are now at or nearly at our target state," and referenced the OCC's removal of Article 17 of the consent order.
  • Mark Mason, CFO, reported, "This quarter, we reported net income of $2.5 billion, EPS of $1.19 and an RoTCE of 5.1% or $19.9 billion of revenues." Mason also stated adjusted net income of $3.6 billion and highlighted that "revenue of $85.2 billion," adjusted for notable items, was "up 7%, our strongest growth in over a decade."

Outlook

  • Mason guided for "NII ex Markets to be up between 5% and 6% in 2026," driven primarily by higher loan volumes in cards and wealth, and deposit growth in services and wealth. He added, "We expect our disciplined expense management combined with top line revenue momentum will drive another year of positive operating leverage as we target an efficiency ratio of around 60% for the full year."
  • Management reiterated the RoTCE target of 10% to 11% for 2026 and a continued focus on capital returns through the $20 billion buyback program. Mason said, "We expect card NCLs to remain within the ranges that we gave for 2025."

Financial Results

  • Mason reported, "On an adjusted basis, which excludes the notable item consisting of the impact of the held-for-sale accounting treatment of Citi's remaining operations in Russia, we reported net income of $3.6 billion, EPS of $1.81 and an RoTCE of 7.7%."
  • Total revenues for the quarter were $19.9 billion, with adjusted revenues up 8%. Expenses for the quarter were $13.8 billion, up 6%, with annual expenses reported at $55.1 billion. The CET1 ratio ended the year at 13.2%, 160 basis points above the regulatory requirement.
  • Services delivered net income of $2.2 billion with an RoTCE of 36.1% in the quarter, while Markets delivered net income of $783 million with an RoTCE of 6.2%. Banking achieved net income of $685 million and an RoTCE of 13.2%. Wealth generated net income of $338 million with an RoTCE of 10.9%. USPB delivered net income of $845 million and an RoTCE of 14.3%.

Q&A

  • Glenn Schorr, Evercore: Asked about flat revenues in Markets despite significant growth in prime balances and trading assets. Mason explained that "a lot of the action that we see in 2025 is on the heels of having spent a lot of time optimizing RWA in the prior years," and that balance sheet is being deployed in higher-returning areas.
  • Michael Mayo, Wells Fargo: Queried progress on transformation and consent orders. Fraser said, "We are operating at almost at our target space. These are the Citi defined ones for compliance, risk and controls. And in data, we've significantly accelerated progress over the past year."
  • Ebrahim Poonawala, BofA: Asked about closing the gap versus peers and investment priorities. Fraser responded that Citi is "investing in scaling our security services platform and broadening capabilities," and aiming to be "top 3 or top 1 in all of the businesses that we're engaged in."
  • Betsy Graseck, Morgan Stanley: Sought detail on the improved NII outlook. Mason pointed to "higher loan volumes that we saw, the higher deposit volumes that we saw throughout the year," and active management to reduce asset sensitivity.
  • James Mitchell, Seaport: Asked about capital return pace. Mason affirmed targeting a 100 basis point management buffer and intention to "look to do more in 2026 in the way of buybacks."
  • Erika Penala, UBS: Inquired about expense savings from consent order completion. Fraser said, "as we complete each body of work, we begin to bring the expenses down related to that."

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts raised probing questions about operating efficiency, transformation progress, and sustainability of capital returns, signaling a neutral to slightly positive tone but emphasizing the need for ongoing execution.
  • Management maintained confident and upbeat prepared remarks, with Fraser stating, "We are now decidedly on the front foot," and Mason asserting, "we are leaving the role not at the peak for Citi, but on the upswing, with nothing but upside from here."
  • Compared to last quarter, analyst tone remains constructive but focused on execution risks, while management's confidence has strengthened as more transformation milestones are achieved.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • Guidance shifted from targeting "less than 60%" to "around 60%" efficiency ratio, reflecting additional investment flexibility for future growth.
  • Management noted a higher adjusted RoTCE in the prior quarter (9.7% vs. 7.7% in Q4), and a step down in adjusted EPS (from $2.24 to $1.81), but highlighted continued revenue growth and capital returns.
  • Analysts' focus remained on transformation progress, operating leverage, and capital allocation, with increased attention to the pace of expense reduction and innovation through AI.
  • Management's tone and confidence levels have increased, with explicit references to being "on the front foot" and signaling visible momentum into 2026.

Risks and Concerns

  • Management acknowledged ongoing work on regulatory data and the need for further validation and regulatory approval, with Fraser stating, "we still do have some work to do."
  • There are macro uncertainties impacting card NCL ranges, but Mason said, "there's nothing that I see right now" in delinquency trends that is concerning.
  • Analysts questioned the impact of potential regulatory changes such as credit card rate caps and the timing of regulatory approvals for Banamex divestiture.

Final Takeaway

Citigroup’s leadership emphasized strong momentum entering 2026, underpinned by robust business performance, continued capital returns, and significant progress in its multiyear transformation. With over 80% of programs nearing target state and disciplined investments in growth and technology—including AI—management remains focused on achieving a 10% to 11% RoTCE and driving another year of positive operating leverage, targeting 5%–6% NII growth in 2026 while completing outstanding regulatory and efficiency milestones.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: Antelope Enterprise receives Nasdaq notice over late interim filing
data.description:
• Antelope Enterprise Holdings Limited (AEHL) on Wednesday said that it has received a letter from the Nasdaq, notifying the company that it is not in compliance with periodic filing requirements due to the late filing of its interim report.
  • The company said Nasdaq determined it failed to timely file a Form 6-K containing interim financial statements for the fiscal half year ended June 30, 2025, as required under Listing Rule 5250(c)(1).
  • Antelope said it has 60 days to submit a plan to regain compliance. If accepted, Nasdaq may grant an extension until June 29, 2026.
  • The notice has no immediate impact on the trading of the company’s shares on the Nasdaq Capital Market.
  • AEHL closed +6.4% at $1.33.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Critical Metals jumps by a third on positive drilling results in Greenland
data.description:
Critical Metals (CRML) closed +32.6% on Wednesday after reporting strong assay results in its 2025 drilling program that confirmed the presence of strategic metals across its Tanbreez rare earths project in Greenland.

The company said the drilling campaign, which focused on the fjord and upper fjord areas of the project, found consistent rare earth mineralization and evidence of strategic metals, including gallium and hafnium, across its Tanbreez asset.

Critical Metals (CRML) said the results reinforce Tanbreez's position as a globally significant peralkaline-hosted rare earth system.

"These results are expected to contribute additional mineralized tonnage to the existing Hill and Lower Fjord Deposits and further support the scale and continuity of the Tanbreez project," Critical Metals (CRML) CEO Tony Sage said.

data.title: Compass Diversified GAAP EPS of -$1.21, net sales of $472.56M
data.description:
• Compass Diversified press release (CODI): Q3 GAAP EPS of -$1.21.
  • Net sales of $472.56M (+3.5% Y/Y).

  • CODI now expects full-year 2025 subsidiary Adjusted EBITDA of $335 million to $355 million, excluding Lugano Holding, Inc.

  • Shares -2.78% AH.

data.title: FuboTV repurchases $140.2M of convertible notes due 2026
data.description:
• FuboTV (FUBO) repurchased $140.2M aggregate principal amount of its outstanding 3.25% convertible senior notes due 2026.
  • The notes were repurchased at 100% of principal plus accrued and unpaid interest, in accordance with the indenture terms.
  • The repurchased notes were originally due on February 15, 2026.
  • The transaction follows Fubo’s receipt of $145M in proceeds from a term loan tied to its 2025 business combination with Hulu + Live TV.
  • Fubo also confirmed that no repurchases were made for its Convertible Senior Notes due 2029 after a fundamental change notice to holders.
  • FUBO shares up 3% post-market.

data.title: S&P Global raises dividend by ~1%
data.description:
• S&P Global (SPGI) declared $0.97/share quarterly dividend, 1% increase from prior dividend of $0.96.
  • Forward yield 0.71%
  • Payable March 11; for shareholders of record Feb. 25; ex-div Feb. 25.
  • The 1.0% increase takes into consideration the expected spin of S&P Global's Mobility division into an independent, public company in 2026.
  • See SPGI Dividend Scorecard, Yield Chart, & Dividend Growth.

data.title: 374Water regains compliance with NASDAQ listing requirement
data.description:
• 374Water (SCWO) on Wednesday said it has regained compliance with Nasdaq’s minimum bid price requirement.
  • The company said Nasdaq notified it that it now meets Listing Rule 5550(a)(2) after its shares maintained a closing bid price of at least $1.00 for 10 consecutive trading days, achieved on January 9, 2026.
  • The exchange has closed the matter, and the stock will continue to trade on the Nasdaq Capital Market.
  • 374Water had previously been notified in January 2025 that it was out of compliance after its shares traded below $1.00 for 30 consecutive business days.
  • SCWO closed -2.32% at $2.52.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Cheniere Energy upgraded at Wolfe as 'fortress in the LNG storm'
data.description:
Cheniere Energy (LNG) +3.1% in Wednesday's trading as Wolfe Research upgraded shares to Outperform from Peer Perform with a $220 price target, citing the stock's compelling valuation even with overbuild headwinds.

Cheniere (LNG) shares have been under pressure amid concerns over an LNG overbuild from a surge of new less contracted U.S. competitor projects and Qatar, but Wolfe analyst Keith Stanley sees the recent weakness as overdone with the stock now trading at its view of floor value based on a conservative distributable cash flow assuming a step-down in contract rates as contracts roll off in 2036 and beyond.

Cheniere's (LNG) 14% free cash flow yield in 2028-29 upon the Corpus 8 & 9 startup is the highest in the sector, and is even more remarkable considering the company is 90% contracted under fixed-fee arrangements for an average of 16 years, with average A- rated credit
counterparties, the analyst said, adding the first contract expiration is not until 2036, by which time LNG could easily have entered an up-cycle.

As negative news flow around competitor project final investment decisions fades this year, Stanley believes investors will re-visit Cheniere (LNG) and see it as an attractive entry point for a high quality equity with undervalued cash flow.

data.title: Tech Voices: DeSantis vs. AI, Tesla's FSD, smart glasses market
data.description:
Seeking Alpha's roundup of statements, announcements, and remarks that could impact the technology sector. 

  • Florida Governor Ron DeSantis has called for more regulation of AI and data centers, which he believes pose a credible threat to the U.S. economy.

“This technology threatens to upend key parts of our economy in ways that can leave many Americans out of work and with consumers footing the bill for the cost of power-intensive data centers,” DeSantis said during his state-of-the-state address, according to Bloomberg. “It presents real perils for children and parents.”

DeSantis has said he is particularly concerned about the strain data centers will put on the electrical grid. 

  • Elon Musk said Tesla (TSLA) is converting its Full Self-Driving technology to a monthly subscription from a one-time fee product.

"Tesla will stop selling FSD after Feb. 14. FSD will only be available as a monthly subscription thereafter," Musk announced on X on Wednesday.

Musk didn't immediately offer a reason for the change.

  • The burgeoning smart glasses market is expected to expand to as much as $200B by 2040, according to HSBC.

Analysts for the bank based their estimate on early strong sales of smart glasses made by eyewear maker EssilorLuxottica (ESLOF) (ESLOY) and Meta (META). They added that the number of smart glasses users is expected to soar from 15M in 2025 to 289M by the end of 2039, with Apple (AAPL), Samsung (SSNLF), or Amazon (AMZN) likely to enter the market in the next couple of years, according to Bloomberg.

“The more competition, the more likely this market will gain critical mass and attract R&D efforts to the benefit of all players,” HSBC analysts said in their note, Bloomberg said.
